# Tools at a glance

← Back to [SKILL.md](../SKILL.md). Every script is described once here, with its
"Run when" routing. Load this file when you need a script's flags, purpose, or
aren't sure which tool fits a step. Tables use compact cells; flag names and
script names are load-bearing — don't drop them.

## Full Restoration — multi-file orchestration (above Stage 1/2/3)

| Tool                                           | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Run when                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`scripts/check-entry.ts`**                   | Sanity-check an entry before building the graph: reads `index.html`, entry out-degree (fan-out) and in-degree (siblings importing it); flags a transitive **vendor leaf** mistaken for an app entry (exit `3`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | **Step 0.5** of [full-restoration.md](../workflows/full-restoration.md), or whenever the entry hash is uncertain. `build-import-graph.ts` runs it automatically.                                                                                                                                                        |
| **`scripts/build-import-graph.ts`**            | BFS from an entry, follow every `import`/`re-export from`; stops at npm-leaf chunks in `resolve-npm-imports.ts`'s `CHUNK_NAME_REGISTRY`. Emits `_full/manifest.json` + stages `_full/files/<basename>/original.js` per local chunk. `--no-entry-check` skips the leaf warning.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | First step of [full-restoration.md](../workflows/full-restoration.md). Re-runnable; preserves prior `stages` + `owner`.                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| **`scripts/make-facade.ts`**                   | Three boundary emitters (detail: [Boundary lifecycle](boundaries.md#boundary-lifecycle--three-terminal-states-never-a-permanent-any-facade)): **default** typed `export declare const … : any` facade (type-check only); **`--reexport <specifier>`** (`--export-star` / `--reexport-named a,b,c`) bare third-party re-export shim (finished, real `@types`); **`--passthrough <ref-relpath>`** `@ts-nocheck` + `// TODO: deep-restore` runtime interim. Reserved-word-safe; `--name-map` supplies/bridges names, `--provenance` sets the header. | Large vendor/runtime-dominated chunk imported by many features: `--reexport` if stock third-party (verify not a fork), else default facade / `--passthrough`. Pair with `ledger.ts mark-faced`.                                                                                                                         |
| **`scripts/build-symbol-ledger.ts`**           | Per `kind: local` file: `extractSymbols`, tag bindings `isExport`/`importedFrom`, assemble `_full/ledger.json` (symbol checklist + cross-file binding table); sets `stages.extracted`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Step 2 of [full-restoration.md](../workflows/full-restoration.md). `--rebuild` blasts progress; default keeps `done` symbols' `restoredName`.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| **`scripts/ledger.ts`**                        | Status-machine CLI: `status`, `next`, `frontier` (every file restorable now — deps `done`/`faced` — for leaf-first fan-out), `claim`/`release` (`O_EXCL` lockfile under `_full/locks/`), `mark-done` (apply renames.json + release + propagate), `mark-faced`, `set-organization` (record domain + semantic path; flip `stages.organized`), `propagate-cross-file [--auto-fill]`, `reset`. Stages `extract → rename → polish → finalize → organize → promote`; `frontier --stage promote` lists promote-ready chunks. Atomic temp+rename writes.  | Throughout the [full-restoration loop](../workflows/full-restoration.md#step-3--iterate-this-is-the-loop). One agent per file × stage; many fan across the `frontier`.                                                                                                                                                  |
| **`scripts/count-direct-compat-exports.ts`**   | Count still-direct compatibility re-exports in aggregator stable-export barrels without crossing unrelated export blocks. Defaults to BUw (`--from ../../app-main-legacy-buw-compat-bundle --legacy-prefix legacyAppMainBuw`); override both flags for other aggregator barrels.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | While draining legacy/current aggregator barrels into semantic modules; use it instead of ad hoc regex snippets to track remaining direct alias counts before and after a restore pass.                                                                                                                                 |
| **`scripts/resolve-direct-compat-exports.ts`** | Resolve direct compatibility re-exports in stable-export barrels back through the bundle tail export table, printing `stable alias -> bundle internal -> public export` plus the internal definition line when found. Defaults to the BUw legacy compat bundle; `--bundle`, `--from`, `--legacy-prefix`, and `--json` support other aggregators.                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Before hand-inspecting a direct aggregator alias. Use it to find the real minified binding definition instead of trusting inherited alias names or manually grepping bundle tail tables.                                                                                                                                |
| **`scripts/auto-restore-full.ts`**             | Batch checkpoint executor: reads manifest + ledger, generates cross-file renames, merges `smart-rename.ts`, assigns fallback names, applies Stage 2 rename + polish, rewrites import/export aliases, fixes lowercase JSX fallback tags, writes flat `.tsx` under `_full/checkpoints/`, emits `_full/auto-restore-report.json` (`needsAgentRewrite: true`).                                                                                                                                                                                       | After graph + ledger init when the tree is too large for hand `renames.json`. Writes only into `_full/checkpoints/` — **never `restored/`**; a mechanical checkpoint only, then run organize → promote. **Do not pass `--write-target-checkpoints`** (deprecated; drops copies into `restored/`, violating staging). |
| **`scripts/plan-organize.ts`**                 | Batch organizer: propose `{ domain, semanticPath, recipe, classification }` per local chunk from shape heuristics (icon → `icons/`, button → `ui/`, single-export → `utils/<kebab>.ts`, vendor/runtime → `boundaries/`; app-features → `needs-review`), surfacing `fallbackRenameRatio`, downgrading collisions. Writes editable `_full/organize-plan.json`; `--apply` writes `manifest.organization` + `stages.organized`.                                                                                                                       | First step of Step 4 in [full-restoration.md](../workflows/full-restoration.md). Review/override (`ledger.ts set-organization`) before `--apply`. `--domain-map` for project-specific domains.                                                                                                                          |
| **`scripts/promote-organized.ts`**             | Promote orchestrator: drains the `promote` frontier (producers first). Per chunk: build typed deliverable (`semantic-finalize` recipe or candidate) → same quality gate → copy into `restored/<domain>/` → upsert `restored/IMPORT_MAP.json` → rewrite producer imports → `stages.promoted`. Gate-fail → rollback + record + continue. Resumable, fleet-safe (`promote` lock), `--dry-run`.                                                                                                                                                      | The step that carries a batch restore out of `_full/` into `restored/`. Run `--dry-run` first; re-run after hand-fixing reported chunks.                                                                                                                                                                                |

## Stage 1 — Deobfuscation

| Tool                                 | Purpose                                                                                                                                 | Run when                                                                   |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`scripts/detect.ts`**              | Classify obfuscation techniques + recommend next steps                                                                                  | First, on anything you suspect is obfuscated                               |
| **`scripts/unpack.ts`**              | Dean Edwards Packer + AAEncode + URLEncode unwrap (iterates layers)                                                                     | Packed/encoded input. Uses `new Function` — gated by `--no-eval`           |
| **`scripts/string-array.ts`**        | Inline Obfuscator.IO `_0x...` string-array refs; remove rotation IIFE + dead array                                                      | Output has `_0x` arrays and indexed lookups                                |
| **`scripts/decode-strings.ts`**      | Decode `\xNN`, `\uNNNN`, `String.fromCharCode(...)`, literal `atob('base64')`                                                           | Hex/unicode escapes or constant base64 strings present                     |
| **`scripts/simplify.ts`**            | Constant folding, dead-code removal, `(0, fn)(...)` → `fn(...)`, backtick-template → string, scope-aware literal inlining (fixed point) | After string-array + decode-strings; also standalone on Rollup/Vite output |
| **`scripts/control-flow-report.ts`** | Detect `while(true){switch}` flatteners + opaque predicates — **report only, no mutation**                                              | Last in Stage 1. Read the report; rewrite by hand                          |
| **`scripts/deobfuscate.ts`**         | Orchestrator: runs every Stage 1 pass in order with `--skip` / `--stop-after`                                                           | One-shot Stage 1. Emits final code + JSON report                           |

## Stage 2 (rename)

| Tool                              | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Run when                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`scripts/sourcemap-check.ts`**  | Detect `//# sourceMappingURL=…` or adjacent `.map`; list original sources                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | **Always first** — if a sourcemap exists, renaming is wasted work                                                                                                                                                  |
| `npx webcrack <file>`             | Unpack webpack/browserify bundles into per-module files                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | One giant bundle (≥ 500 KB or many `id: (e,t,n)=>{}` modules)                                                                                                                                                      |
| **`scripts/wakaru-normalize.ts`** | Pre-rename mechanical decompiler wrapping external `npx @wakaru/cli` (Rust) — recovers ES6 classes, async/await, optional chaining, destructuring, TS enums, etc. (~66 rules polish lacks). **NOT a deobfuscator or semantic renamer**; auto-skips when the binary is absent. `--level standard` default (`minimal` for fidelity-critical, `aggressive` only with verification); `--dce` opt-in; `--unpack` single-bundle only. | **Readable tier: default-on**, after sourcemap-check + detect (+ Stage 1 if obfuscated), **before** `extract.ts`; skip when a usable `.map` exists. Deep mode: per chunk body only, never `--unpack` a split tree. |
| **`scripts/extract.ts`**          | Parse JS, emit every binding + scope context as JSON. Filters (`--kind`, `--scope-kind`, `--min-refs`, `--only-cryptic`, …) trim huge files.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Step 1 of rename pipeline                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| **`scripts/plan.ts`**             | Group symbols into ordered rename batches + emit `CHECKLIST.md`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | When symbols.json has > ~200 entries                                                                                                                                                                               |
| **`scripts/smart-rename.ts`**     | Deterministic rename for mechanical cases — React `props` params, event handlers, `.map`/`.reduce` callbacks, hook returns. Id-keyed JSON for `apply.ts`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | **Before** judgment-naming params (Pass 3); covers ~80 % of the boring cases. Detail: [naming-heuristics.md](naming-heuristics.md)                                                                                 |
| **You (the agent)**               | Read symbols, write new names to JSON (one batch at a time for big files)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Step 2 of rename pipeline; whatever `smart-rename.ts` couldn't decide                                                                                                                                              |
| **`scripts/apply.ts`**            | Apply a rename map in one scope-aware AST traversal; collision-safe, reserved-word safe, and export/import alias preserving even for very large rename sets                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Step 3 of rename pipeline                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| `npx prettier --write`            | Reformat output                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | After apply/polish, for different formatting than `@babel/generator` gave                                                                                                                                          |

**External tools (not bun deps):** `wakaru-normalize.ts` shells out to `npx @wakaru/cli@1.5.0` (or a global `wakaru` / prebuilt binary). wakaru is a **Rust** binary invoked as a subprocess (like `webcrack`/`prettier`), **not** added to `package.json`. The wrapper degrades gracefully (passthrough + stderr note) when unavailable, so the core bun+Babel path never depends on it.

## Stage 2 (polish, post-rename)

**Reading-aid subset (`--fast`, default tier):** `strip-react-compiler`, `simplify`, `jsx-runtime`, `inline-defaults`, `normalize-exports` — change how code _reads_. **Import-resolution tail (deep mode only):** `react-shim-elim`, `resolve-npm-imports`, `npm-cjs-shim-elim`, `dead-shim-elim` — only make imports resolve against `node_modules`. The default tier runs `polish.ts --fast` and stops at the subset.

| Tool                                  | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Run when                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **`scripts/strip-react-compiler.ts`** | Strip React Compiler memoization scaffolding (`let cache = react.c(N)` + every `cache[i] === x ? read : compute` conditional)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Renamed file has `react.c(N)` or `(0, x.c)(N)` cache scaffolding                                                                                                                               |
| **`scripts/simplify.ts`**             | Same as Stage 1; post-rename collapses `(0, x.y)(...)` → `x.y(...)`, `` `svg` `` → `"svg"`, `{a: a}` → `{a}`, `{true:"X",false:""}[cond?…]` → `cond && "X"`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | Renamed file still has `(0, fn)(...)`, backtick literals, or long-form properties                                                                                                              |
| **`scripts/jsx-runtime.ts`**          | Convert `jsxRuntime.jsx("svg", {…})`, `jsxs` / `Fragment` calls back to JSX                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | React build (Vite/Rollup/esbuild) with JSX compiled to runtime calls                                                                                                                           |
| **`scripts/inline-defaults.ts`**      | Inline `let X = p === undefined ? D : p` / `let X = p ?? D` into destructure defaults `{ p = D }`; merge `let A, B; ({A, B} = expr);` into `let { A, B } = expr;`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Output has separate destructure + resolved-default declarations                                                                                                                                |
| **`scripts/normalize-exports.ts`**    | Collapse `var X = expr; export { X as Y };` (+ function/class variants) into `export const Y = expr;`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Tree-shaken aliased exports — common in Rollup/esbuild bundles                                                                                                                                 |
| **`scripts/react-shim-elim.ts`**      | Collapse React CJS namespace shims (`toESM(loadReact())`) to `import React from "react"`; prune now-unused runtime imports                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | After JSX conversion, before npm resolution, when a file imports both a runtime helper chunk and a `jsx-runtime` chunk                                                                         |
| **`scripts/resolve-npm-imports.ts`**  | Rewrite hashed chunk imports (`./clsx-DDuZWq6Y.js`, `./jsx-runtime-XXXX.js`) to bare specifiers (`clsx`, `react/jsx-runtime`); split mixed npm/local `./shared` re-exports; rename `_React` → `React`                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Rollup/Vite bundles where vendored npm lives alongside project code                                                                                                                            |
| **`scripts/npm-cjs-shim-elim.ts`**    | Collapse leftover `toESModule(defaultImport(), 1)` namespaces after npm resolution into the bare npm default import                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | After `resolve-npm-imports.ts`, before `dead-shim-elim.ts`, on wrappers like `var dom = toESModule(ReactDOM(), 1)`                                                                             |
| **`scripts/dead-shim-elim.ts`**       | Final pass — iteratively removes top-level `var X = lazyY()` with 0 refs whose callee is an unused import specifier (Vite/Rolldown lazy-namespace); drops orphaned specifiers/imports; strips a trailing stale `//# sourceMappingURL=…`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Always last in `polish.ts`. No-op on hand-written files. Loops to a fixpoint so cascades collapse in one run.                                                                                  |
| **`scripts/format.ts`**               | Shell out to `bunx prettier --write` (or `npx`). File or directory (`**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,mjs,cjs}`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Final pipeline step, after `polish.ts` (and multi-export split)                                                                                                                                |
| **`scripts/polish.ts`**               | One-shot Stage 2 polish: strip-react-compiler → simplify → jsx-runtime → inline-defaults → normalize-exports → react-shim-elim → resolve-npm-imports → npm-cjs-shim-elim → **dead-shim-elim**. `--rename` runs `smart-rename` + `apply` first (so `polish.ts <file> --rename --fast --format` is the default-tier one-shot). `--fast` skips the import-resolution tail (**deep mode only**). Defaults `--prefer local`; `--format` chains prettier.                                                                                              | `--rename --fast` for the readable tier; the full chain (no `--fast`) in deep mode to resolve npm imports                                                                                      |
| **`scripts/vendor-npm-preflight.ts`** | Focused guard for `restored/vendor/*`: `--decision` classifies the edit target before the file exists (`npm-shim` vs `needs-proof`); `--intent local-body` blocks both public npm targets and nested vendor edits while the containing vendor root has any npm-shim failure; normal mode reuses `quality-gate.ts` npm-shim rules and reports only `third-party-npm-shim-*` failures (stock package body not re-exported from npm, missing package dependency, or a bare re-export that names members absent from the installed package runtime). | Before editing run `--decision --intent local-body` or `--decision --intent npm-shim` on the target file; before committing run normal mode on `restored/vendor` or the whole `restored` tree. |

## Stage 3 (semantic finalize)

| Tool                               | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Run when                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ---------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`scripts/semantic-finalize.ts`** | Recipe finalizer. `--recipe icon` → typed `IconProps` modules (multi-icon → directory + `index.ts` barrel); `--recipe button` → named variant tables, typed props, `forwardRef`, `displayName`, default export; `--rewrite-imports --import-map map.json` → consumers from bundle aliases (`t`, `n`) to semantic named imports + split barrel paths.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | After `polish.ts`, before final formatting. For pure SVG icons, small multi-icon chunks (`expand-XXXX`), Button/control chunks (`button-XXXX`).                                                                                                                                       |
| **`scripts/plan-split.ts`**        | Draft `split-plan.json` for multi-export modules from exports + registry objects. A starting point, not semantic grouping.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Before `split-bundle.ts` on any file with ≥ 3 exports, a registry object, or > 1000 lines                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| **`scripts/split-bundle.ts`**      | Execute a reviewed split plan: bindings → directory, cross-file imports for helper refs, `index.ts` barrel, rebuild registry-object exports                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | After Stage 2 polish / semantic naming, before final format/gate, for multi-export bundles                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| **`scripts/promote-final.ts`**     | Gate-before-copy: runs `quality-gate.ts` on a candidate, optional report, then replaces the target. Not final acceptance — Stage 3 review still follows.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Instead of `cp` when moving a host-rewritten candidate into the target                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **`scripts/quality-gate.ts`**      | Hard finalization gate: fails on missing provenance, too many cryptic params/bindings, cryptic public exports, generated fallback names, dense one-letter refs, stale runtime/compiler residue, suspicious lowercase JSX tags, or flat files that should split. On a full-restoration target also audits `_full/manifest.json` + `IMPORT_MAP.json` / legacy `chunks` + `boundaries` and rejects app-feature chunks that are missing, mechanical, placeholders, facades, `@ts-nocheck`, `oversized-local`, or not Stage 3 accepted — and a **third-party npm boundary still left as an `any`-facade** (`full-restoration-npm-boundary-not-resolved`; convert via `make-facade.ts --reexport`). `--check-format` runs `prettier --check`. `--vendored` (and auto-detected `make-facade.ts` output) relaxes semantic checks for faithful vendored modules / facades. | Before copying a polished file, and again after final split/format. Whole-tree deep/full: final run must be `quality-gate.ts <target-dir> --check-format`, not a boundary-only check. Non-zero exit ⇒ partial deliverable. `--allow-missing-provenance` only for known intermediates. |

**Import map default:** when rewriting imports for files in the same restored project, pass the shared map:

```bash
IMPORT_MAP="restored/IMPORT_MAP.json"   # one shared map at the restore root
bun <skill-dir>/scripts/semantic-finalize.ts --rewrite-imports <file-or-dir> \
  --import-map "$IMPORT_MAP"
```

Append new hash→semantic entries as the project grows; reuse across future chunks so consumers don't accumulate duplicate maps. The map is always `restored/IMPORT_MAP.json`, regardless of which entry triggered the restore. For delta/boundary replacements, derive rewrites from the existing map (semantic local imports over hashed paths; unresolved runtime/vendor boundaries keep the documented facade path until restored).

## Stage 3 (acceptance review) — deep mode; default = optional naming review

| Tool                                    | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Run when                                                                                                                                             |
| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`scripts/prepare-stage-e-review.ts`** | Batch the deep-mode public tree into review packets under `<target>/.deobfuscate-javascript/_stage-e` (`index.json`, `batches.tsv`, `README.md`, `batch-XX.md` with checklist + inlined sources). Excludes checkpoints. `--verify` checks packets still cover the tree exactly once. **Optional aid** whether the host reads them or a sub-agent does. | Large deep-mode tree, after `quality-gate.ts` passes, to batch the read. `--max-files` / `--max-bytes` size batches. Not required for small targets. |
| **`scripts/acceptance-checklist.md`**   | The four-category (E1–E4) quality bar each file is read against — exemplar Button, strict verdict format, common-false-positive list. Host applies it during self-review; can also seed an optional `Agent(subagent_type=general-purpose, …)` call. Markdown, not a script.                                                                            | Every deep-mode review, as the grading standard. Detail: [stage-3-finalize.md](../stages/stage-3-finalize.md).                                          |
| **`scripts/verify-stage-e-results.ts`** | Parse saved sub-agent replies under `_stage-e/results/`, enforce the strict verdict-block format, fail unless every `index.json` file has exactly one `PASS` and no `NEEDS_FIX`.                                                                                                                                                                       | **Only when an optional reviewer sub-agent produced verdict files.** Host self-review needs no results files; its record is the Stage 3 summary.     |

For delta restores, Stage 3 acceptance scope is the changed public file set. If the packet helper emits the whole tree, prepare a small changed-file packet or review only the changed batch and report scope accurately; don't present a partial delta review as all-tree acceptance proof.

## Exit codes

All scripts use BSD-style exit codes so they compose cleanly in shell pipelines:

| Code | Meaning                                                    |
| ---- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| `0`  | Success                                                    |
| `1`  | I/O error (missing input file, write failure, etc.)        |
| `2`  | JavaScript parse error                                     |
| `64` | Usage error (missing arg, unknown flag, unknown step name) |

`sourcemap-check.ts` additionally exits `1` when no recoverable sourcemap is found — that's not a failure, it's "proceed with the rename pipeline".
